personal.introduction: I'm a first year Information Technology student who likes figuring out how things are built, then rebuilding them a little cleaner. Right now that means learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript one small project at a time.

about.biography: I'm Prian Andrei D. Gallardo, a first year BSIT student who got into computers by taking apart the family desktop more times than anyone was comfortable with. Somewhere between reinstalling the OS and figuring out why the Wi-Fi kept dropping, I realized I liked the troubleshooting more than the panic.
about.background: Before college I spent a lot of time in Canva and Google Sites making layouts for school projects, which is probably why I lean toward the design side of development. I didn't touch real code until my first semester, so most of what's on this site was learned in the last few months.
interests: Editing short videos for friends' projects
about.whyBSIT: I chose BSIT because I wanted a course that let me build things people can actually use, not just theory on paper. Web development felt like the fastest way to see an idea turn into something clickable.
about.careerGoals: Long term, I want to work as a front-end developer or UI/UX designer for a company that takes accessibility and clean design seriously. Short term, I just want to finish this course with a portfolio I'm not embarrassed to show people.

projects.title: Task Slayer
projects.description: A cooperative, terminal-based multiplayer game built in Java, where players team up to fight bosses by typing prompts correctly and quickly before the boss attacks.

projects.title: UN.BOUND Design System
projects.description: A streetwear e-commerce UI/UX case study with a full light and dark design system, covering 20 screens from browsing to checkout, designed in Figma.

projects.title: BuildWeb Landing Page
projects.description: A responsive promotional landing page for a web design course, built from a written spec and matched pixel-by-pixel against reference screenshots.

projects.title: This Portfolio
projects.description: A JSON-driven personal portfolio site, where the JavaScript layer reads all content from a data file so the page updates without touching the HTML.

education.program: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
education.experience: Currently taking foundational courses in web development, data structures, and UI/UX design. Learning to balance coursework theory with building real, working projects.
It was mainly because i thought there wasnt an ICT strand in SHS lol, which led directly into choosing BSIT for college.
